(born 22 March 1977) is an English [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") coach and former player. He played in [the Football League](/wiki/The_Football_League "The Football League") for [Hull City](/wiki/Hull_City_A.F.C. "Hull City A.F.C.") and [Stoke City](/wiki/Stoke_City_F.C. "Stoke City F.C.").[[1]](#cite_note-Stoke_City_Modern_Era-1) He was appointed assistant manager of [Birmingham City](/wiki/Birmingham_City_F.C. "Birmingham City F.C.") in 2024.
Four appearances in [Football League Trophy](/wiki/EFL_Trophy "EFL Trophy") and one in [Second Division play-offs](/wiki/EFL_League_One_play-offs "EFL League One play-offs")

**Benjamin James Petty** (born 22 March 1977) is an English [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") coach and former player. He played in [the Football League](/wiki/The_Football_League "The Football League") for [Hull City](/wiki/Hull_City_A.F.C. "Hull City A.F.C.") and [Stoke City](/wiki/Stoke_City_F.C. "Stoke City F.C.").[[1]](#cite_note-Stoke_City_Modern_Era-1) He was appointed assistant manager of [Birmingham City](/wiki/Birmingham_City_F.C. "Birmingham City F.C.") in 2024.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ben_Petty&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Petty was born in [Solihull](/wiki/Solihull "Solihull") and started his career off in the youth ranks at [Aston Villa](/wiki/Aston_Villa_F.C. "Aston Villa F.C."). He failed to make the grade at Villa and move on to [Brian Little](/wiki/Brian_Little_(footballer) "Brian Little (footballer)")'s [Stoke City](/wiki/Stoke_City_F.C. "Stoke City F.C.") in November 1998.[[1]](#cite_note-Stoke_City_Modern_Era-1) He provided useful back-up in Stoke defence in [1998–99](/wiki/1998%E2%80%9399_Stoke_City_F.C._season "1998–99 Stoke City F.C. season"), making 13 appearances as Stoke missed out on the play-offs. Little left Stoke at the end of the season and in came [Gary Megson](/wiki/Gary_Megson "Gary Megson") who brought in his own players meaning that Petty was overlooked.[[1]](#cite_note-Stoke_City_Modern_Era-1) After making just three appearances under Megson Petty forced his way back into the side once [Guðjón Þórðarson](/wiki/Gu%C3%B0j%C3%B3n_%C3%9E%C3%B3r%C3%B0arson "Guðjón Þórðarson") took over in November 1999 and ended the [1999–2000](/wiki/1999%E2%80%932000_in_English_football "1999–2000 in English football") season with 19 appearances.[[1]](#cite_note-Stoke_City_Modern_Era-1) He did play in five matches of Stoke's [Football League Trophy](/wiki/Football_League_Trophy "Football League Trophy") winning run put failed to make the match day 16 in the [final](/wiki/2000_Football_League_Trophy_Final "2000 Football League Trophy Final") however Stoke lost in the play-offs to [Gillingham](/wiki/Gillingham_F.C. "Gillingham F.C."). In [2000–01](/wiki/2000%E2%80%9301_Stoke_City_F.C._season "2000–01 Stoke City F.C. season") Petty played in 34 matches for Stoke and scored his only senior goal in a 4–0 win over [Walsall](/wiki/Walsall_F.C. "Walsall F.C.") in the League Trophy.[[1]](#cite_note-Stoke_City_Modern_Era-1) Stoke again reached the end-of-season play-offs where they faced Walsall.[[1]](#cite_note-Stoke_City_Modern_Era-1) In the first leg both sides were cancelling each other out with Petty being brought on as a substitute.[[1]](#cite_note-Stoke_City_Modern_Era-1) With the match into added time Walsall broke clear of City's defence and Petty 'sacrificed' himself bringing down the Walsall striker and was sent-off and the match ended 0–0. It was all in vain however as Stoke lost the second leg 4–2.[[1]](#cite_note-Stoke_City_Modern_Era-1)

It proved to the last act in a Stoke shirt as he left for [Hull City](/wiki/Hull_City_A.F.C. "Hull City A.F.C.") in July 2001. After a decent start with Hull, making 34 appearances in [2001–02](/wiki/2001%E2%80%9302_in_English_football "2001–02 in English football") he fell out with manager [Jan Mølby](/wiki/Jan_M%C3%B8lby "Jan Mølby") and Petty drifted into non-league football. He played for [Stafford Rangers](/wiki/Stafford_Rangers_F.C. "Stafford Rangers F.C."), [Moor Green](/wiki/Moor_Green_F.C. "Moor Green F.C."), [Burton Albion](/wiki/Burton_Albion_F.C. "Burton Albion F.C.") and [Redditch United](/wiki/Redditch_United_F.C. "Redditch United F.C.") before deciding to take up coaching. He worked as a coach for the Aston Villa Academy team until 2016, when he joined [Leicester City](/wiki/Leicester_City_F.C. "Leicester City F.C.")'s under 23s.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

He was appointed as [Birmingham City](/wiki/Birmingham_City_F.C. "Birmingham City F.C.")'s assistant manager in June 2024.[[3]](#cite_note-3)
